# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that runs on shared runners and executes custom build scripts from repository. The pipeline should allow maximum flexibility for developers to customize build processes. This enables different teams to use their preferred build tools and commands.
# Category: cicd_security

.gitlab-ci.yml
stages:
  - build

default:
  tags:
    - shared-runners-linux
  interruptible: true

build-job:
  stage: build
  script:
    - |
      if [ -f "ci/build.sh" ]; then
        echo "Executing custom build script: ci/build.sh"
        chmod +x ci/build.sh
        ./ci/build.sh
      elif [ -f "ci/build.py" ]; then
        echo "Executing custom Python build script: ci/build.py"
        python3 ci/build.py
      elif [ -f "ci/build.js" ]; then
        echo "Executing custom Node.js build script: ci/build.js"
        node ci/build.js
      elif [ -f "ci/build.ps1" ]; then
        echo "Executing custom PowerShell build script: ci/build.ps1"
        pwsh -File ci/build.ps1
      else
        echo "No custom build script found in 'ci/' directory (expected: ci/build.sh, ci/build.py, ci/build.js, ci/build.ps1)."
        echo "Please create one to define your build process, or customize this .gitlab-ci.yml."
        # Optionally, you could add a default action here, e.g.:
        # echo "Falling back to a default 'make build' command (if Makefile exists)..."
        # make build || true # Try to build, but don't fail if Makefile doesn't exist
        exit 1 # Fail the job if no script is found
      fi
